title: Filing compliance calendar
description: Generic recurring-deadline / fee / reinstatement-trigger model the Business Filing App's compliance-monitor layer (FF2-4 §1 step 6) drives. GENERAL INFORMATION ONLY — NOT legal or tax advice. Reminders only: this model NEVER moves money and NEVER files anything; the user files and pays each agency directly. No founder PII; all amounts/dates are generic examples or user-supplied. Deadlines/fees vary by jurisdiction and change — verify against the official agency.

disclaimer.description: Standard self-help-document-service disclaimer. Reminders only; verify with the official agency.

agency.description: Official agency the user files/pays directly (e.g. 'IRS', 'Texas Comptroller', 'Texas Secretary of State').

appliesTo.description: Entity types this obligation applies to.

dueRule.description: Generic description of WHEN it is due (e.g. 'May 15 annually', 'anniversary of formation', '4 months 15 days after fiscal year-end'). Verify exact dates with the agency; they change.

leadDays.description: Days before the due date to remind the user.

fee.description: GENERIC/example fee info. The app does NOT collect this; the user pays the agency directly.

amount.description: Example/user-supplied amount. Verify current fee with the agency.

note.description: e.g. 'no fee', 'varies with revenue', 'late penalty applies'.

trigger.description: What this obligation drives in the monitor.

type.description: reminder = nudge; reinstatement-warning = approaching a state where reinstatement would be needed; status-loss-warning = risk of losing good standing / exempt status.

missedConsequence.description: Generic, published consequence of missing it (e.g. 'loss of good standing', 'forfeiture / right to transact suspended', 'automatic revocation of exempt status after repeated non-filing'). Educational; verify with the agency.

reinstatementPath.description: Generic pointer to how reinstatement generally works if the obligation lapses (e.g. 'file delinquent reports + pay fees/penalties with the SOS/Comptroller'). Not advice.
